description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a heavy, dark metal head. The handle is made from a light-colored wood, showing visible grain and ergonomic shaping for a comfortable grip. The axe head is broad with a single sharp cutting edge and a pronounced, downward-curving pick or spike on the opposite side, making it suitable for both chopping and prying tasks. The blade is securely mounted to the handle, with the wood passing through the eye of the head and appearing tightly fitted. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, focusing on durability and function. This type of axe is commonly found in urban settings such as garages, workshops, or maintenance sheds.
description: A sturdy hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a heavy, dark metal head featuring a sharp blade and a hooked spike. The design is practical and built for chopping and prying tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the profile of the metal head with its blade and hooked spike.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the thickness of the axe head and the rectangular cross-section of the handle where it meets the head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curvature and the reverse profile of the axe head, including the spike.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the cutting edge of the blade and the width of the head, with the handle extending backward.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with ergonomic shaping, visible grain, and an oval cross-section for improved grip.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, broad steel blade with a sharp cutting edge and a pronounced hooked spike on the opposite side; no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the wooden handle passing through the eye of the axe head.
